preamble: The shift from a palatal glide [j] to a voiceless postalveolar fricative [ʃ] involves fricativization and devoicing. This process is seen in regional variations of Portuguese (like in some Carioca pronunciations of /j/) and in certain stages of French palatalization.

examples.note: Devoicing and fricativization of palatal glides in certain dialects.
Historical stage in the development of palatalized consonants.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
Watson, J. C. (2002). The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic.
